mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive forward returns exceed 55% when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances or t-statistic falls below 1.5 over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 50% of cases or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output that boosts short-term market sentiment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power during tightening cycles with Rank IC dropping below 0.01 or returns are negative in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent and core inflation remains elevated, the probability of aggressive central bank tightening rises, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in > 60% of triggered observations or the interaction effect reverses sign in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent annually signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of low-growth quarters or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 2-year windows or positive returns drop below 50% when payrolls increase.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines while the consumer price index exceeds 200, stagflationary pressures emerge that force risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive returns in > 55% of cases or the predictive accuracy falls below random chance over a 4-year out-of-sample period.
